name: self-upgrade
description: Check for and install the latest evm version from PyPI
scenario: A user wants to check whether a newer evm release is available on PyPI and upgrade if so. Demonstrate the check-only path (no changes), the dry-run preview, and the actual upgrade. Show how to branch on exit codes (1 = update available, NOT an error), handle network-unreachable gracefully, and use --json for scripting.
success_criteria: Uses `evm upgrade --check` to check without installing
Correctly interprets exit 1 from `--check` as 'update available' (not a failure)
Correctly interprets exit 0 from `--check` as 'already up to date'
Uses `--dry-run` to preview the pip command before a real upgrade
Handles network-unreachable (latest reported as unknown, exit 1) gracefully rather than failing loudly
Uses `--json` for programmatic consumption of current/latest/update_available
Explains that `evm upgrade` runs `pip install --upgrade evm-cli` with the current interpreter
Notes that `evm upgrade` does not trigger shell-integration auto-install
Does NOT treat `--check` exit 1 as an error to suppress or panic about
expected_commands: if evm upgrade --check; then echo "up to date"; else echo "update available"; fi
anti_patterns: Treating `evm upgrade --check` exit 1 as an error (it means 'update available')
Running `evm upgrade` directly in automation without `--check`/`--dry-run` first
Assuming the upgrade can set variables in the parent shell (unrelated, but a common confusion)
Ignoring network-unreachable as a recoverable case
Forgetting that `upgrade` skips auto-install (no need to set EVM_NO_AUTO_INSTALL)
